Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Data availability lists Twb5-A accession ON376002.

Source_Species.reason: The paper identifies Twcytb5 genes as from Tripterygium wilfordii.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Tw genes studied are from Tripterygium wilfordii.

Applied_Species.reason: Twcytb5-A was coexpressed in both Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both species are explicitly used as heterologous hosts for Twcytb5-A experiments.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Twcytb5-A is explicitly described as a cytochrome b5.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The abstract states Twcytb5-A increases triptonide output more than 9-fold in S. cerevisiae, and Results show it substantially increases CYP-product accumulation, especially in yeast.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Results state six Twcytb5 genes were tested, only Twcytb5-A substantially increased 8, effects were stronger in yeast, and effects in N. benthamiana could be mixed/negative for some intermediates.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by coexpression in engineered S. cerevisiae and transient Nicotiana benthamiana assays with GC-MS/LC-qTOF-MS; six Twcytb5s were compared in yeast.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Triptonide is the pathway end product discussed for the engineered system including Twcytb5-A.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Triptonide is explicitly a diterpenoid/triepoxide diterpenoid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Introduction states triptonide is a bioactive triepoxide diterpenoid with pharmaceutical potential including non-hormonal male contraceptive activity.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Twcytb5-A is coexpressed with TwCYP82D274, TwCYP71BE85, TwCYP71BE86, and in the complete pathway with TwCYP82D213; TwPOR1 is the reductase used in yeast alongside TwCYPs.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper presents Twcytb5-A as improving pathway output in yeast and supporting fermentation production, directly supporting engineering value.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: A major result is host-dependent behavior in S. cerevisiae versus N. benthamiana.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results explicitly note negative effects of Twcytb5-A on 8, 6, and 7 accumulation in certain Nicotiana combinations, implying host-dependent imbalance/electron partitioning effects.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Twcytb5-A was heterologously coexpressed in yeast and tobacco leaves.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper mentions public T. wilfordii RNA-seq deposits for candidate CYP discovery, but does not clearly state Twcytb5-A itself was discovered from those omics data; accession numbers are not given there.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper frames cyt b5 as an additional electron donor and states only Twcytb5-A among the tested cytochrome b5 proteins had the strong stimulatory effect.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Data availability lists TwPOR1 accession ON376008.

Source_Species.reason: TwPOR1 is identified by BLAST in T. wilfordii transcriptomes.

Applied_Species.reason: TwPOR1 was integrated into Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Saccharomyces cerevisiae is explicitly the applied host.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: TwPOR1 is explicitly described as a gene encoding CYP reductase.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The Results say TwPOR1 was integrated to support downstream CYP functionality in yeast, enabling pathway reconstruction to intermediates and triptonide.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points align with Results: identified by BLAST, used as reductase partner in yeast, and supportive rather than main substrate-specific subject.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: TwPOR1 was genome integrated with TwCYPs in S. cerevisiae and function inferred from metabolite production.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: TwPOR1 is part of the yeast system producing triptonide.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper explicitly states triptonide has pharmaceutical potential including male contraceptive activity.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: TwPOR1 was used together with TwCYP82D274, TwCYP71BE85, TwCYP71BE86, and TwCYP82D213 in the yeast pathway, and Twcytb5-A is discussed as an additional redox partner in the same system.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Using TwPOR1 as the required redox partner for heterologous CYP function in yeast is directly supported.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The context is explicitly the heterologous yeast expression system.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Engineered yeast expression/genome integration is explicitly described.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper states TwPOR1 was identified by BLAST searches in T. wilfordii transcriptomes.

Other_Important_Info.reason: It is presented as a general CYP electron donor supporting the heterologous pathway rather than determining product specificity.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Data availability lists TwCYP82D274 accession ON376001.

Source_Species.reason: TwCYP82D274 is a T. wilfordii gene.

Applied_Species.reason: The gene was tested in Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both host species are explicitly stated.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The gene name itself is TwCYP82D274, a cytochrome P450.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is explicitly a CYP from the CYP82D subfamily.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The Results place TwCYP82D274 in triptonide biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: It is described as performing early oxygenation of miltiradiene leading toward downstream abeo-abietane/triepoxide products.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: TwCYP82D274 is one of the four CYPs required for triptonide production.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Triptonide's pharmaceutical and contraceptive potential is stated in the introduction.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show it depletes miltiradiene and forms 14-hydroxy-dehydroabietadiene (5); it is part of the minimal four-CYP set for triptonide formation.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Its role is clearly upstream/early-to-mid in pathway reconstruction.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states TwCYP82D274 oxygenates miltiradiene-derived substrate leading to 14-hydroxy-dehydroabietadiene and discusses C-ring oxygenation.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Coexpression assays directly tested turnover of miltiradiene (3).

Primary_Product.reason: 14-hydroxy-dehydroabietadiene (5) is the explicit main product observed and consensus product across hosts.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: It forms an early intermediate used for downstream rearrangement and epoxidation to triptonide.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements match the Results section.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states candidate genes were isolated from cDNA derived from root, stem and leaf tissue.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The gene was heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana leaves and engineered yeast.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Triptonide is native to T. wilfordii and also produced in the heterologous hosts in this study.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Its necessity for pathway reconstruction in heterologous hosts is directly shown.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: It is combined experimentally with TwCYP71BE85, TwCYP71BE86, TwCYP82D213, TwPOR1, and Twcytb5-A.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper notes possible endogenous N. benthamiana contributions and states no prior CYP82D had been shown to oxygenate diterpenoids to their knowledge.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Individual/combinatorial expression in both hosts with GC-MS/LC-qTOF-MS is explicit; NMR identified downstream products in the system.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Calling it a key upstream enzyme is supported, and the caveat that explicit rate-limiting status was not established matches the paper.

Omics_Data.reason: Candidate genes were identified from public T. wilfordii RNA-seq data deposits.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Host-dependent differences between N. benthamiana and S. cerevisiae are explicitly described.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper documents host-dependent product differences and broad product promiscuity/shunt products.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Data availability lists TwCYP71BE86 accession ON375999.

Source_Species.reason: It is a T. wilfordii gene.

Applied_Species.reason: The gene was tested in Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Enzyme_Name.reason: TwCYP71BE86 is explicitly a cytochrome P450 gene of this designation.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is explicitly assigned to the CYP71BE subfamily.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: It is part of the triptonide biosynthetic pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Its role is in A-ring rearrangement and contributes to lactone-forming steps toward the abeo-abietane scaffold.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: TwCYP71BE86 is one of the four CYPs required for triptonide production.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states triptonide has pharmaceutical potential including male contraceptive activity.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results and Discussion explicitly state TwCYP71BE86 catalyzes the unprecedented 18(4→3) methyl shift and contributes to formation of rearranged intermediates and, with TwCYP71BE85, triptophenolide.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: It is a midstream branch-defining scaffold rearrangement step.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper directly supports A-ring oxygenation-associated carbon rearrangement to the abeo-abietane core and accumulation of 6 and 7; contribution to 8 with TwCYP71BE85 is also supported.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper supports that TwCYP71BE86 can act in combination with TwCYP82D274 on product 5 and also that it can accept 3 as substrate in planta, but the exact primary substrate is unresolved.

Primary_Product.reason: Compounds 6 and 7 were identified as products associated with TwCYP71BE86 expression, and 8 is produced in combination with TwCYP71BE85.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The rearranged scaffold is explicitly described as essential for downstream triptonide biosynthesis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed findings are explicitly supported by Results and Discussion.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: It was isolated from T. wilfordii cDNA and close homologs were found in tandem repeats in the genome.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Heterologous expression in tobacco leaves and engineered yeast is explicit.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper supports native accumulation of related abeo-abietane diterpenoids in T. wilfordii and heterologous accumulation in yeast/tobacco, but this field mixes native terminal product with related compounds.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Its critical scaffold-diversifying role for engineering triptonide/abeo-abietane pathways is directly supported.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: TwCYP71BE86 is used experimentally with TwCYP82D274, TwCYP71BE85, TwCYP82D213, TwPOR1, and Twcytb5-A.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly proposes a Wagner-Meerwein-type rearrangement and broad product promiscuity/multiple catalytic cycles.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by combinatorial heterologous expression in both hosts with metabolite profiling and NMR structure elucidation.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: It is clearly a key branch-defining scaffold-establishing step, though not quantitatively proven rate-limiting; the wording accommodates that.

Omics_Data.reason: The CYP library was identified from public T. wilfordii RNA-seq data.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Host-dependent product distribution and enhancement by Twcytb5-A in yeast are explicitly reported.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper discusses broad promiscuity and shunt products from incomplete oxygenation/rearrangement cycles.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Data availability lists TwCYP71BE85 accession ON375998.

Applied_Species.reason: The gene was tested in Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both applied host species are explicit.

Enzyme_Name.reason: TwCYP71BE85 is the cytochrome P450 designation used in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is explicitly a CYP71BE subfamily member.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: It is one of the four CYPs required for triptonide biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Its role is in A-ring oxidation/lactone-forming steps.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: It is linked to triptonide as part of the minimal four-CYP pathway.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Triptonide's pharmaceutical and contraceptive potential is stated.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support that TwCYP71BE85 works with TwCYP71BE86 and is required in yeast for lactone ring formation leading to 8.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Its role is midstream in forming 8 before terminal triptonide formation.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper supports contribution to A-ring oxygenation/lactone formation in cooperation with TwCYP71BE86, especially from yeast evidence.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The exact substrate for TwCYP71BE85 is not directly identified; it is inferred to act on TwCYP71BE86-generated or related intermediates.

Primary_Product.reason: Triptophenolide (8) accumulation is significantly increased when TwCYP71BE85 is coexpressed with TwCYP71BE86 and is required in yeast for 8 formation.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: 8 is presented as a pathway intermediate en route to triptonide.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements match the Results section.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: It was isolated from T. wilfordii cDNA.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves and engineered yeast is explicit.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The field broadly references native downstream diterpenoids and heterologous accumulation, but specific accumulation site information for this gene-product relation is not directly stated.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: It is necessary for efficient pathway reconstruction and 8/triptonide formation in heterologous systems.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: It is tested with TwCYP82D274, TwCYP71BE86, TwCYP82D213, TwPOR1, and Twcytb5-A.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed indicates its clearest role is host-supported lactone formation with TwCYP71BE86, and substrate order remains unresolved.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Combinatorial heterologous expression with LC-qTOF-MS/GC-MS and NMR-supported metabolite identification is described.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: It is fair to call it a key mid-pathway enzyme while noting rate-limiting status was not established.

Omics_Data.reason: The CYP candidate library came from public T. wilfordii RNA-seq data.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Promiscuity of TwCYPs generally is discussed, but specific side-product tradeoffs attributable to TwCYP71BE85 alone are not clearly demonstrated.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Data availability lists TwCYP82D213 accession ON376000.

Source_Species.reason: It is a T. wilfordii gene.

Applied_Species.reason: The gene was tested in Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Enzyme_Name.reason: TwCYP82D213 is the enzyme designation in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is explicitly in the CYP82D subfamily.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: It is part of the minimal triptonide biosynthetic gene set.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Its described role is a late B/C-ring epoxidation-related completion of triepoxide formation.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: TwCYP82D213 is directly linked to triptonide formation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Triptonide's bioactivity and male contraceptive potential are explicitly stated.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results explicitly state TwCYP82D213 completes the biosynthetic pathway to triptonide when coexpressed with the other three TwCYPs.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Its role is downstream/terminal in the reconstructed pathway.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper supports a role in late oxygenation/epoxidation steps together with TwCYP82D274, though exact chemistry remains somewhat inferred.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The exact substrate is not directly demonstrated; 8 or related intermediates are plausible but not explicitly proven.

Primary_Product.reason: Coexpression of TwCYP82D213 with the other three CYPs resulted in accumulation of triptonide (2) in both hosts.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: It is directly connected to formation of the target product.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary statements are directly reflected in Results.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: It was among T. wilfordii cDNA-isolated CYP82D genes.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: It was heterologously expressed in tobacco leaves and engineered yeast.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Triptonide is native to T. wilfordii and produced heterologously here, but the wording about native tissues plus tobacco leaves is broader than directly shown for this gene-specific field.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Its necessity as the terminal CYP for triptonide platform reconstruction is directly supported.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: It is coexpressed with TwCYP82D274, TwCYP71BE85, TwCYP71BE86, with TwPOR1 in yeast, and pathway output is enhanced by Twcytb5-A.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion supports highly coordinated epoxidation cycles and absence of observed mono-/diepoxide intermediates.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Combinatorial heterologous expression in both hosts with LC-qTOF-MS detection of triptonide is explicitly described.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: As a terminal determinant for triptonide formation, this is directly supported; no stronger unsupported rate claim is made.

Omics_Data.reason: The candidate CYPs were identified from public T. wilfordii RNA-seq data.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Triptonide formation with this gene was confirmed in both tobacco and yeast.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The discussion supports that late epoxidation may require highly coordinated catalysis and inefficient turnover may lead to shunt/quinone products, though partly inferential.
